a:r.a:t: DH allows two parties to derive the same shared secret over a public channel without ever transmitting the secret itself. An eavesdropper observing all traffic still cannot compute the shared_secret without solving the discrete logarithm problem.
any modification breaks the key and decryption fails completely.
Why All Parameters Must Be Bound
Ensures only A & B can compute it
Binds key to specific device identities
Ensures key is time-bound and unique
Change any one parameter → different key → decryption fails completely
The session key is never transmitted
it is independently derived by both
